entries.description: Set 030 initialized: session-state v3 sessions ledger + terminology alignment (5 sessions). Proposal authored by/with GPT-5.4, strong-approved by Gemini Pro. Operator-locked decisions: complete-everywhere terminology unification, regex-first AI-fallback migration, on-activation detection trigger, Setting-up-your-project loading state.

Set 030 spec revised per GPT-5.4 implementation review (2026-05-17). Five changes applied: (1) final publish moved from S4 to S5 (after migration UX ships) - D14; (2) dual-write kept as operational steady state, stop-legacy deferred to future set gated on consumer v3-reader confirmation - D5 revision; (3) terminology drift resolved by updating proposal doc to use 'complete' instead of 'done' globally - D2 alignment; (4) lint rule narrowed to 'no application reader may read legacy fields except through approved compat helpers' - D13; (5) spec-title-extraction routing config registered in S1 instead of S5 - removes dependency risk. Added D15 (Set 029 ordering: both orders supported) and D16 (Lightweight ergonomics dry-run vs accessdb state file before publish).

Read existing schema doc, examples, proposal, session_state.py, types.ts, router-config.yaml, test_session_state_v2.py to map baseline state of v2 schema, existing test conventions, and extension wiring. Confirmed proposal terminology was already 95% aligned to 'complete'; identified two lingering 'done' references at lines 139 and 246 that need patching per D2.

Created ai_router/progress.py (~530 LOC) with ProgressView + SessionRecord dataclasses, SessionStateInvariantError exception, canonicalize_status alias map, extract_session_titles_from_spec regex helper, synthesize_v3_from_v2 read-time normalizer, get_progress + validate_invariants enforcing all 8 invariants. Pure functional (no filesystem coupling beyond the spec.md read in extract_session_titles_from_spec).

Created ai_router/tests/test_progress.py covering v3 happy paths (fresh/in-flight/between/complete/cancelled), each of the 8 invariant violations with rule-number assertions, v2 synthesis (in-flight, between, complete-consistent, complete-with-missing-fails-loud, alias canonicalization, spec-missing fallback, bool/float type confusion), title extraction (4 cases), canonicalizeStatus (8 parametric cases), and direct validator entry with top_status=None. 40 → 48 tests after Round A.

Created tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/utils/progress.ts (~440 LOC) as line-for-line TS mirror of progress.py. Updated tools/.../src/types.ts adding SessionStatus, SessionRecord, ProgressView, SessionStateV3 interfaces (kept legacy SessionState 'done'|'in-progress'|... type alias for the extension's bucketing layer until Session 3 migrates it). Created tools/.../test/suite/progress.test.ts mirroring the pytest suite shape (mocha tdd). 38 → 46 tests after Round A. tsc --noEmit clean.

Rewrote docs/session-state-schema.md from v2 to v3. New sections: 'v3 is canonical; v2 read support is permanent', 'Reader contract — every reader uses get_progress()', 'v3 schema shape', 'sessions[] — the canonical progress ledger', 'Dual-write legacy fields (Set 030 steady state)', 'Status — the canonical glossary' with the Set 030 terminology note retiring 'Done' from display labels, 'Derived values — the get_progress() view', the 8 invariants with explicit rule-2-tightened-to-contiguous-from-1 note, Lightweight one-field-flip worked example with every transition, v3 worked examples (not-started/mid-set/between-sessions/complete), tier expectations, v2 read compat path with default-to-not-started rule explanation, bucketing rules.

Rewrote docs/session-state-schema-example.json to v3 closed shape (schemaVersion: 3, sessions[] with 4 entries all complete, dual-write legacy triple still present). Rewrote docs/session-state-schema-example.md with v2-vs-v3 side-by-side narrative, lifecycle shapes (not-started/in-progress/complete), and regenerator workflow notes. Patched lingering 'done' references in docs/proposals/2026-05-17-...md at lines 139 and 246 to 'complete' per spec D2.

Registered spec-title-extraction task type in ai_router/router-config.yaml per spec D14 (removes Session 5 dependency risk). Added task_type_overrides entry pinning to gemini-flash; task_type_scores entry at 20 (low/mechanical band); per-model task_type_params with thinking off for cost predictability. Created ai_router/tests/test_spec_title_extraction_registered.py with 6 guard tests asserting the routing wiring stays correct (score in low band, override pins tier-1 model, per-model params present for all 6 pool models, pick_model resolves via override, NOT in always_route_task_types, NOT in auto_verify_task_types).

Ran full pytest suite (testpaths = ai_router/tests, -m 'not e2e'): 476 passed, 1 skipped, 8 e2e deselected. No regressions in existing suite from Session 1's additions. Confirmed test_dump_session_state_schema.py is broken pre-existing (import issue, predates Session 1).

Round A verification: gpt-5-4 via ai_router.route(task_type='session-verification'). Initial Round A attempt with ~2000 LOC bundle (Python+TS helpers + tests + types + config) hit 3-attempt read timeout. Retried with tighter scope (Python helper only, ~514 LOC) per memory feedback_split_large_verification_bundles. Cost: $0.275525, elapsed 213s, in=5138 out=17512 tokens, no escalation. Verifier returned REJECTED with 6 must-fix issues.

Applied all 6 must-fix issues from Round A: (1) removed synthesize_v3_from_v2 force-promote-on-complete (now defaults to not-started; rule 7 surfaces contradiction); (2) added strict-int filter (Python: type(v) is int; TS: Number.isInteger + typeof !== boolean) to reject bool/float in v2 currentSession/completedSessions[]; (3) tightened rule 2 to contiguous-from-1 per spec D12 (rejects [1, 3] and [2, 3]); (4) removed 'cancelled' from SESSION_STATUSES (top-level still accepted); (5) hoisted rule 8 above the top_status None-guard so it always fires; (6) changed unknown top-level status from rule 5 to rule 2 (shape/enum). Updated 8 pytest cases + 8 mocha cases for new behavior. Updated schema.md, example.md to document tightened invariants and fail-loud synthesizer.

Re-ran full pytest suite after Round A fixes: 484 passed, 1 skipped, 8 e2e deselected (+8 vs pre-Session-1). Mocha progress.test.ts: 46/46. TypeScript tsc --noEmit: clean. No regressions in any pre-existing tests.

Registered Session 2 start via python -m ai_router.start_session. Snapshot flipped to currentSession=2, completedSessions=[1] preserved from Session 1 close-out. work_started event #2 appended to session-events.jsonl.

Bumped SCHEMA_VERSION 2->3. Added 5 v3 helpers to session_state.py (_existing_sessions_records, _spec_titles_for_set, _build_sessions_array, _derive_legacy_fields, _validate_sessions_or_raise). Re-exported SessionStateInvariantError + 3 session-status constants + helpers from progress.py so writers raise the same exception class the read-side validators use. Updated _migrate_v1_to_v2_inplace gate to literal '>= 2' so v2 files pass through on read (v3 promotion is the writer's job).

register_session_start rewritten to dual-write per spec D5: builds v3 sessions[] via _build_sessions_array, validates via _validate_sessions_or_raise BEFORE any file write or event emission (per spec D6 + Round-A fix #4), then derives the legacy triple via _derive_legacy_fields. effective_total fallback: caller-supplied -> existing state -> max(spec_titles, completed, session_number). Includes Round-A fix #1 fail-loud rejection of session_number > effective_total and prior completed > effective_total.

_flip_state_to_closed rewritten to dual-write. Resolves totalSessions through the fallback chain; raises SessionStateInvariantError if still 0 (Round-A fix #3). Splits forced (incident-recovery promotes 1..total to complete) from natural close (uses new_completed as-is; validator catches gaps - Round-A fix #2). Always builds v3 sessions[], always validates before write.

_not_started_payload now includes a v3 sessions[] array when totalSessions is known from spec.md (all entries not-started). Per rule 1, sessions[] is omitted only when totalSessions is unknown ('any set with a known plan'). _backfill_payload promotes the array's per-session statuses to match the inferred top-status: change-log present -> all complete; activity-log only -> session 1 in-progress (conservative); neither -> all not-started. All scaffolding paths run _validate_sessions_or_raise.

Updated test_session_state_v2.py for v3 dual-write semantics: schemaVersion assertions bumped 2->3; test_writes_v3_with_lifecycle_state asserts sessions[] present with session 1 in-progress + others not-started + derived legacy triple; test_writes_closed_lifecycle_state restructured to close sessions 1 and 2 naturally (without force=True) so the final state passes invariants; test_mark_complete_rewrites_v1_as_current_schema updated to assert forced=True now promotes all 5 sessions to complete (incident-recovery semantic) + forceClosed=True marker.

Updated 3 downstream tests for v3 currentSession semantics (strictly the in-progress session's number, or None when no session in-flight - replaces v2's ambiguous 'in-flight OR most-recently-closed'): test_happy_3session.test_happy_3session_full_cycle (currentSession is None after every close); test_force_close_path.test_force_close_nonfinal_session (completedSessions=[1,2,3] under forced incident-recovery, not [1,2]); test_close_session_snapshot_flip.test_close_session_multi_session_set_clean (currentSession is None after final close).

Created ai_router/tests/test_session_state_v3.py (~720 LOC). 37 mocha-style test cases covering: SCHEMA_VERSION; _build_sessions_array unit tests (status assignment, title carry-forward, generic fallback, rejection of invalid total); _derive_legacy_fields unit tests; register_session_start v3 dual-write (sessions[] + legacy triple, title carry-forward across spec.md mutation, dual-write parity at session 2 start, idempotent work_started, totalSessions backfill); mark_session_complete v3 dual-write (mid-set keeps SET in-progress, final close flips to complete, forced promotes all); scaffolding writes v3 (synthesize_not_started_state, ensure_session_state_file, backfill_session_state_files); writer-rejects-out-of-range (Round-A fix coverage); flip-requires-totalSessions; natural-close-does-not-promote-all; SessionStateInvariantError re-export identity.

Ran full pytest suite after writer changes + test updates: 522 passed, 1 skipped, 8 e2e deselected (+38 vs pre-Session-2). 3 downstream failures identified and fixed inline (currentSession-after-close semantic shift). TypeScript tsc --noEmit clean (no TS-side code changed; Session 3 owns the reader migration).

Round A verification: gpt-5-4 via ai_router.route(task_type='session-verification'). Focused slice of session_state.py (~770 LOC across 7 ranges per memory feedback_split_large_verification_bundles). Cost: $0.266495, elapsed 242s, in=13058 out=15590 tokens, no escalation. Verifier returned REJECTED with 4 must-fix issues: (1) silent truncation of out-of-range session numbers; (2) _flip_state_to_closed promotes all on natural close (not just forced); (3) unvalidated legacy-only fallback write when totalSessions unknown; (4) work_started event emitted before invariant validation.

Applied all 4 must-fix issues from Round A: (1) _build_sessions_array now raises rule 2 if in_progress_number or any completed_numbers fall outside [1, total]; register_session_start raises rule 2 if session_number > effective_total or max(prior_completed) > effective_total; (2) _flip_state_to_closed now splits forced (promote 1..total to complete) from natural close (completed_for_array = new_completed; validator surfaces any gap) at lines 807-843; (3) removed legacy-only fallback write; if totalSessions can't be resolved through fallbacks, raise rule 1 at lines 790-797; (4) reordered register_session_start: build sessions[] -> validate -> emit work_started -> write snapshot (was: emit -> build -> validate -> write). Added 7 regression tests covering each fix in test_session_state_v3.py.

Re-ran full pytest suite after Round A fixes: 529 passed, 1 skipped, 8 e2e deselected (+7 vs pre-Round-A from new regression tests). TypeScript tsc --noEmit clean. No regressions in any pre-existing tests.

Round B verification: gpt-5-4 via ai_router.route(task_type='session-verification'). Same slice strategy as Round A; updated line ranges for the post-fix file (~870 LOC across 7 ranges). Cost: $0.193638, elapsed 151s, in=13249 out=10701 tokens, no escalation. Verifier returned VERIFIED with explicit line-by-line confirmation for each of the 4 Round A issues plus 'no new contradiction' assessment per fix.
